NXT-65088.summary: MP - Menu/ Menu Cycle Analysis - Update and Combining Grains and MMA for Daily and Weekly (SBP 14+)
NXT-65088.rn_title: Menu and Menu Cycle Analysis: Grains and MMA Updates
NXT-65088.ac:
* On *meal patterns page*, update the Component of Grains to Grains or Meat/Meat Alternate (oz. eq.) and no longer display Meat/Meat Alternate in the list
* On the *Standard Menu* page, remove the toggle for Use as Grains for MMA for breakfast as this is no longer a requirement to specify
* On the *menu analysis*, update the Component of Grains to Grains or Meat/Meat Alternate (oz. eq.) and no longer display Meat/Meat Alternate in the list
** Slide-out
** Nutrition Report
* On the *Menu Cycle Analysis*, update the Component of Grains to Grains or Meat/Meat Alternate (oz. eq.) and no longer display Meat/Meat Alternate in the list
** Slide-out
** Nutrition Report
** Food Component Report
* Calculations for this will be a combination of both components together (Use same logic as before but combine the calculations)
** Continue to calculate the grains 80% WGR rule on any grains offered within the combined totals
*** Per USDA “School may offer grains, meats/meat alternates, or a combination of both to meet the daily and weekly ounce equivalents for this combined component. At least 80 percent of grains offered weekly at breakfast must be whole grain-rich”

** 6. Schools may substitute 1 oz. M/MA for Grains after the minimum daily Grains requirement is met.
** 7. The daily requirement for Grains must be met before substitution of M/MA for Grains is allowed.
** 8. USDA is offering flexibility by allowing SFAs to exceed the maximum amount of grains and meat/meat meal alternates as long as the daily and weekly minimums for these components are met and the calories are within the required range.

NXT-65369.summary: MP - Menu/Menu Cycle Analysis - Consider Product Based Added Sugars (CACFP)

NXT-65369.ac:
* Check menu/menus to find any menu items that have “Product Based Added Sugar” Flag
* Based on what type of Product, analyze to ensure compliance is met
** Breakfast cereals may have no more than 6 grams of added sugars per dry ounce
** Yogurt may have no more than 12 grams of added sugars per 6 ounces (2 grams of added sugars per ounce).
* Pass/Fail Added Sugar and provide a alert for user to check what menu item is causing the fail
** Only Display Added Sugar as the nutrient value as required IF Product based flag is available for any item on the menu 
*** Remove % of calories column for all nutrient values as there is no requirement to calculate % of calories
**** This applies for both menu and menu cycle analysis
* Add additional changes as part of added sugar requirement into analysis documentation
* Update all reports that include nutrients as well as the slide out

** Nutrient Summary (Weekly)
* Provide a disclaimer on all appropriate pages “Product based sugar compliance for CACFP has been implemented as of Oct 1, 2025 for breakfast cereals & yogurt”

Any breakfast cereals or yogurts marked as a “Product Based Sugar” items will now be checked for added sugar and flagged on all analysis if the added sugar exceeds the amount allowed.

NXT-69848.summary: Menu Analysis - Site Performance - Planned Accuracy Metric & Slide out
NXT-69848.ac:
* On Site Performance page, provide a new column for Planned Accuracy 
** This will be after the Served Portions Column
* The calculation for Planned Accuracy is (Served/Planned)*100= Planned Accuracy
* Provide an icon to allow user to open a slideout from the column to show calculations
* When user clicks on a particular row on the slideout, update the calculation on the bottom of the slide out to reflect the numbers and show math “work”
* Allow sorting on the slideout
* Freeze Calculation at the bottom and formula at the top, also freeze header row and allow scrolling for the sites
* Freeze main page Site and Totals header
* Provide button at the bottom right for “Go back to the top” to allow users to get to the top of the sites section
NXT-69848.desc:
As a district manager taking a look at my site performance on workspace, I would like to be able to see how well my managers planned vs what was actually served.

!image-20260130-222557.png|width=671,alt="image-20260130-222557.png"!

NXT-69849.summary: Menu Analysis - Site Performance - Produced Accuracy Metric & Slide out
NXT-69849.ac:
* On Site Performance page, Rename Production Accuracy to Produced Accuracy 
** This will be after the Planned Accuracy Column
* The calculation for Produced Accuracy is (Served/Produced)*100= Produced Accuracy
* Provide an icon to allow user to open a slideout from the column to show calculations
* When user clicks on a particular row on the slideout, update the calculation on the bottom of the slide out to reflect the numbers and show math “work”
* Allow sorting on the slideout
* Freeze Calculation at the bottom and formula at the top, also freeze header row and allow scrolling for the sites
NXT-69849.desc:
As a district manager taking a look at my site performance on workspace, I would like to be able to see how well my managers Produced vs what was actually served.

!image-20260130-222915.png|width=1019,alt="image-20260130-222915.png"!

NXT-69862.summary: Menu Analysis - Scoring Metric Update - Include Reach Score in Overall Scoring
NXT-69862.ac:
* Introduce new scoring metric called Reach Factor which will include both # of days menu item was served and how many schools it was served at 
* [https://cybersofttechnologies-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/lalita_jandhyala_primeroedge_com/IQBNh1fq4yapRr5yxpmn7SwtAXJk-_1UsJZ4QJKujr0C4Sg?e=sTfoq8|https://cybersofttechnologies-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/lalita_jandhyala_primeroedge_com/IQBNh1fq4yapRr5yxpmn7SwtAXJk-_1UsJZ4QJKujr0C4Sg?e=sTfoq8|smart-link] includes all the calculations for how this will be achieved
* This should effect all calculations moving forward based on filter of date range selected by the user and rescoring all items and School performances based on that
NXT-69862.desc: As a District manager looking at my menu analysis scoring, I think it makes sense to factor in how many days a menu item was served and to how many schools it was served at in a particular time period. I also want a way to filter out menu items based on the number of days or schools it was served.

NXT-69863.summary: Menu Analysis -Item Performance - Provide Filter sliders for Days Served and Schools Served
NXT-69863.ac:
* On Item Performance, provide a filter that allows users to filter by min and max Days Served and Schools Served
* Both filters will be affected by the Date range filter, Schools Filter and Categories filter
* Update the filter sliders to include what the min and max are based on the other filters selected
* Allow user to use slider to select minimum or maximum number of days served
** Provide a text box to allow them to type/select if user does not want to use slider
* Allow user to use slider to select minimum and maximum number of schools served
** Provide a text box to allow them to type/select if user does not want to use slider
* Default filters to the minimum for both 
** If user already filtered on the dashboard, inherit those filters on the Item Performance list
* Hold filters if they return to the page from the menu item drill down and return to the menu items page
* Update the Participation (% ) to Production Accuracy (% ) and use the formula (Served/ Produced)*100 = Produced Accuracy
NXT-69863.desc:
As a district manager looking at my menu items, I would like to have a way to filter items out based on the number of days served and the number of sites it was served to in a particular time frame.

!image-20260203-045655.png|width=911,alt="image-20260203-045655.png"!

NXT-70459.summary: MP - Menu Versioning - Version Slide out
NXT-70459.ac:
* Provide slideout for versions to be able to see what version have been made for a menu
** Current version should always be on the top and cascade down in order
* Display Number of production records and assigned menus that are associated with each version 
** Provide the effective date for the particular version 
* Provide an icon for a user to view a particular version in an overlay that leads them to the new standard menu page ([NXT-70460|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-70460])
* Provide an icon that triggers a choice for the user to restore a version or create a new menu ([NXT-70461|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-70461])
* On the list page, provide options to view all the versions including current 
** Leads them into view mode of the current menu
NXT-70459.desc:
As a menu planner, when new versions have been created for my menu, I would for a way to go back and few any historical version and see what was on it at the time.

*Description:*                                                                                                                                                                                      
  Display a Menu Analysis bar on the Menu Detail page (View Mode) between the Menu Header and Menu Items sections, as shown in the attached mockup image.                                                                                                       
 

*Requirements* (refer to attached image):

# Analysis bar shows two rows — a header row with column labels and a data row with values
# Columns: Menu Analysis label, Calories, Sat. Fat, Sodium, Carbs, Fruit, M/MA, Grain, Milk, Veg, and Analysis result
# Data row values are displayed as solid colored chips — green for Pass, orange for High/Fail, darker orange for Exceeded — with white text (not outlined boxes)
# Legend chips (Pass, High/Fail, Exceeded) appear in the first column of the data row
# Food component columns (Fruit, M/MA, Grain, Milk, Veg) show colored circle icons next to the label, with values in brackets like [10.00]
# Last column shows overall PASS or FAIL with an eye icon and the serving group name, e.g., "Analysis (K-5)"
# Serving group selection is synced — when a user clicks a serving group column header (e.g., PK(25), K-5(200)) in the Menu Items grid below, the Analysis bar updates to show that serving
group's data. The first serving group should be pre-selected on page load so both the grid column and the analysis bar are active by default.

  API: GET /api/Menus/{menuId}/Analysis — returns analysis data per serving group. 



**Use Mock Data for now **

*Version Restore functionality is not working as expected* 


*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Navigate to *Menu Planning → Menu*
# Open an existing menu
# Update menu data and save
# Observe that the menu version changes (e.g., V1 → V2)
# Open the *Version Slide-out panel*

The system should restore the menu data to version V1.

*Actual Result:*
The system does not restore V1 data, the current version remains unchanged.

** Applies to all Meal Types

*** Separate Reduced Fat and remove the 2% indication and also provide Flavored and Unflavored 
** Applies to all Meal Types
